name: ATS
recent_news: No recent M&A or news found; YellowPages lists ATS at 7627 Dahlia St as a school bus service. Website ride-ats.com unreachable at time of research (ECONNREFUSED). Distinct from OH-based broker 'ATS Transportation Services Inc' (DOT 2227585).
fit_notes: Very small (~9 employees, sub-$1M revenue) Commerce City CO operator with school + SPED tags. Sub-scale for Summit tuck-in on a stand-alone basis; could be a micro bolt-on if Summit builds a Denver-metro hub, but limited information available and ambiguous identity (ATS acronym shared by multiple unrelated CO entities).

name: Independent International Bus
recent_news: No web footprint found; no website, no FMCSA hits under this exact name, no press. Broken Arrow Public Schools runs its own transportation in-house, suggesting this entity likely services a niche route or private/charter school work rather than a district contract.
fit_notes: Single-tier (~10 employees, <$0.5M revenue) micro-operator founded 2016 by an individual owner. Almost certainly sub-scale and not a meaningful Summit tuck-in target; flag only if Summit is consolidating Tulsa-metro micro-fleets at near-asset values.

name: Quantum Care Solutions Inc
recent_news: Operates DBA 'Able Care' as authorized passenger carrier (DOT 4329332 / MC 1690140); marketed as 'transportation for the disabled' / non-emergency medical transport (NEMT) van service in Louisville KY. No M&A activity found. Distinct from unrelated Houston-based healthcare IT firm at quantumcaresolutions.com.
fit_notes: NEMT/disability van operator, NOT a school-bus contractor — does not fit Summit's core student-transportation tuck-in thesis. Skip unless Summit is building a paratransit/SPED-adjacent NEMT arm.

name: Romeo Limousines
recent_news: No web presence, BBB listing, Yelp page, or FMCSA snapshot found for 'Romeo Limousines' in Fernley NV. PO Box mailing address and limo-style branding suggest a sole-proprietor livery operator rather than a school-bus contractor.
fit_notes: Limousine / private livery micro-operator — not a school-bus or student-transportation contractor. Not a Summit tuck-in target; exclude from working buyer-side list.

name: Arrow Handy Cap Transport
recent_news: NPI 1598856262 (enumerated Sep 2006) lists the address C8 Calle Hermogenes Figueroa, Carolina PR as 'Arrow Medical Transport' — a ground/land ambulance service owned by Noel Medina Gonzalez Sr. under Puerto Rico license TC AMB 145. The 'Arrow Handy Cap Transport' name appears to be an alternate trade name for the same micro-ambulance operation, not a school-bus contractor.
fit_notes: Puerto Rico ground-ambulance / handicap-van micro-operator — not a school-bus contractor and outside Summit's mainland CONUS footprint. Not a tuck-in target; exclude.
